sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the current image, the white "P" character has moved DOWN and RIGHT, and is now adjacent to the blue "U". The character is now at rows 44-52, cols 34-51 (approximately). The blue "U" is at rows 53-58, cols 50-58.
I can see the white "P" is now touching the blue "U" on the right side. The red blocks are visible at the overlap region.
Let me check the current state to see if the level is closer to being completed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("=== Objects ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'R', 'N', 'Y', 'b']:
        b = n['boundary']
        minr = min(r for r,c in b)
        maxr = max(r for r,c in b)
        minc = min(c for r,c in b)
        maxc = max(c for r,c in b)
        print(f"id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} bbox=({minr},{maxr},{minc},{maxc})")

# Check adjacency for white
print("\n=== Adjacency (W objects) ===")
for pair in seg['adjacency_list']:
    i, j = pair
    ci = next((n['color'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['id'] == i), '?')
    cj = next((n['color'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['id'] == j), '?')
    if ci == 'W' or cj == 'W':
        print(f"{i}({ci}) - {j}({cj})")

# Count red blocks
red_count = sum(1 for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'R')
print(f"\nRed block count: {red_count}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the white "P" (id=13) is adjacent to the blue "U" (id=17). The blue "U" now has 27 pixels (was 45), so 18 blue pixels were replaced by the character's white color.
The white "P" is at rows 50-58, cols 35-52. The blue "U" is at rows 53-58, cols 53-58.
The white "P" is now connected to the blue "U". It's no longer connected to the green bar or yellow "P".
So it seems like the character can only connect to one object at a time. The goal might be to connect to each object sequentially, and the level completes when all connections are made.
But wait, the level hasn't completed yet. Let me think about what else needs to happen.
Looking at the red blocks, they seem to be "connection points" that indicate where the character needs to connect. The white "P" has red blocks at its "feet" (bottom in upright orientation, left in rotated orientation).
Let me re-examine the pattern. In Level 1, the goal was to move the character to fit into the green rectangle's hole. In Level 2, the goal might be to move and rotate the character to connect with all three objects (green bar, yellow "P", blue "U").
But the character can only connect to one object at a time. So maybe the goal is to connect to each object in a specific order, or to connect to all objects simultaneously by positioning the character at a specific location.
Let me try rotating the character to see if it helps connect to multiple objects at once.
Actually, looking at the current state, the white "P" is at rows 50-58, cols 35-52. The blue "U" is at rows 53-58, cols 53-58. The white "P" is to the left of the blue "U".
The green bar is at rows 14-34, cols 38-49. The yellow "P" is at rows 35-52, cols 17-31.
If I rotate the character, it might be able to connect to multiple objects. Let me try rotating.

<parameter=code>
# Let me try rotating the character to see if it helps connect to multiple objects
action(['SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
